methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Anduril Industries, a Series H defense and security company founded by Palmer Luckey, Brian Schimpf, Trae Stephens, Matt Grimm, and Joe Chen, commands a current valuation of $78B+ following $11B+ in total capital raised. The Defense & Security sector contains 23 same-sector peers, comprising 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private ones, including Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, Chaos Industries, and Skydio.

Peer-median valuation methodologies proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow peer clustering. While the sector universe exists, no same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparable set yielded the three-datapoint minimum required for robust triangulation. An all-sector median approach would introduce material measurement error given the heterogeneity of defense subsectors and the absence of direct-stage analogs at Anduril's scale and maturity.

The $78B+ valuation reflects the capital intensity, regulatory barriers, and customer concentration inherent to defense technology development. Key uncertainties include the absence of standardized exit multiples in early-stage defense private markets, the timing and terms of potential public market transitions for comparable firms, and the difficulty benchmarking a company of Anduril's absolute capital accumulation against traditional VC-backed cohorts. Further valuation refinement would require either materialization of same-stage public comps or disclosure of late-stage defense private fundraising terms.
